behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is in parsing/segmentation logic itself, within one function boundary. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess required; input is a command string. MOCK-HIDE: Mocks are not relevant because no external dependency is needed. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Integration because behavior text is only about segmentation output, not cross-module handoff. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: N/A (Unit is lowest).
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmentation function with a multi-line command block (assignments + command). Assert it returns discrete ordered segments per newline statement, and does not return one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This incident is a handoff bug between segmentation and trigger classification; unit-testing classifier alone with pre-segmented input can miss it. REAL-INFRA: No real subprocess/network needed to classify trigger text. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking segmentation could hide the exact newline-block regression. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: No agentic chain. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess dependency for correctness, (b) no AI output, (c) no multi-agentic sequence, (d) no LLM-governed orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because behavior text requires recognition of command text, not execution of `gh` or HTTP.
behaviors.test_description: Run the PostToolUse detection pipeline end-to-end in-process with raw multi-line input containing assignments then `gh pr create`. Assert classification is PR-create trigger (not `not_a_trigger`).

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: False-positive risk arises from interaction of segmentation + command-type classifier on multi-line blocks; unit tests of one piece can miss boundary behavior. REAL-INFRA: No real subprocess/network required for this classification decision. MOCK-HIDE: Stubbing parser output can mask misclassification caused by newline handling. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess dependency, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because behavior concerns static trigger discrimination between `pr` and `issue`, not runtime `gh` execution.
behaviors.test_description: Execute detection pipeline with a multi-line block mirroring the PR case but ending in `gh issue create`. Assert result is not PR-create trigger and does not open review loop.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Unit tests for identifier parsing alone miss failures in wiring parser output to state persistence format/path. REAL-INFRA: Needs local filesystem persistence, but not OS-level primitives beyond normal file I/O; this fits Integration. MOCK-HIDE: Mocked persistence could hide serialization/key/path contract bugs. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real network/subprocess behavior, (b) no AI output, (c) no agentic chain, (d) no LLM-stage handoff. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because behavior text asks for reconstruct+persist contract, not OS-specific failure modes (permissions, file locks, watchers). LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): a pure unit test of reconstruction would pass even if persisted state omits/mangles PR identifier field.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a detected PR-create event through PostToolUse state-writing path using a temporary test filesystem. Assert persisted review-loop state includes the reconstructed PR identifier in expected field/type.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is a cross-module contract (PostToolUse writer -> PreToolUse reader/enforcer); unit tests on either side can miss schema/version/key mismatches. REAL-INFRA: Local filesystem handoff is sufficient; no real external API required. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking reader or writer independently can hide true compatibility failures. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: No agentic pipeline. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no mandatory real subprocess/network condition in behavior text, (b) no AI dependency, (c) no agentic sequence, (d) no LLM orchestration handoff. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because behavior requires interoperability of state format and enforcement logic, not OS-specific runtime behavior. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): writer unit tests and reader unit tests can both pass while combined contract still fails (e.g., field name mismatch).
behaviors.test_description: In one test, run PostToolUse detection/write path to emit state, then invoke PreToolUse enforcement path against that state file. Assert PreToolUse successfully reads state and enforces review gate for subsequent protected command.
